title: Python Programming Basics
description: Start coding with Python. Learn the syntax, variables, data types, and control flow structures needed to write basic programs. This course requires absolutely no prior programming experience.

seo_metadata.title: Python Programming Basics Certificate Course | ProCerix
seo_metadata.description: Learn Python programming from scratch. Master syntax, variables, loops, and functions. Start your coding journey today.

learning_outcomes: Install Python and write your first script
Understand variables and basic data types (strings, integers, floats, booleans)
Use control flow structures (if statements, loops)
Work with fundamental data structures (lists and dictionaries)

modules.title: Introduction to Python
modules.description: Getting set up and writing your first lines of code.

lessons.title: Hello World
lessons.content:
Your First Program

Python is known for its readability. The first program anyone writes is printing text to the screen: print("Hello, World!")

lessons.title: Variables and Data Types
lessons.content:
Storing Information

Variables are containers for storing data values. Python has no command for declaring a variable; it is created the moment you first assign a value to it. Main data types include strings (text), integers (whole numbers), floats (decimals), and booleans (True/False).

modules.title: Control Flow
modules.description: Making decisions and repeating actions in your code.

lessons.title: If...Else Statements
lessons.content:
Logic and Decisions

Python relies on indentation to define scope. An if statement evaluates a condition, and executes the indented code block only if the condition is True. elif (else if) and else handle alternative conditions.

lessons.title: For and While Loops
lessons.content:
Iteration

A while loop executes a set of statements as long as a condition is true. A for loop is used for iterating over a sequence (like a list, a tuple, a dictionary, a set, or a string).

lessons.title: Writing Functions
lessons.content:
Reusable Code

A function is a block of code which only runs when it is called. You can pass data, known as parameters, into a function. Functions are defined using the def keyword.

lessons.title: Lists and Dictionaries
lessons.content:
Collections

A List is a collection which is ordered and changeable, written with square brackets []. A Dictionary is a collection which is ordered, changeable and do not allow duplicates, written with curly brackets {} and stores data in key:value pairs.
